rationale: Keep: the citing paper’s core construct is decision type (choosing vs. rejecting) and it explicitly positions itself as extending choosing-versus-rejecting research to repeated decisions, while the cited 2000 JMR paper is directly about additive vs. subtractive (add/delete; choose/reject-like) option framing effects. Given this strong theoretical/mechanism overlap and a confirmed DOI-level reference match (10.1509/jmkr.37.2.187.18731), this is plausibly foundational rather than an incidental mention.
